metadata.title: Rare Diseases: Common Issues in Drug Development — Guidance for Industry

rules.rule_text: Approval of any drug for either a rare or a common disease or condition must be based on substantial evidence of the drug's effectiveness for its intended use and sufficient information to conclude that the drug is safe for use under the conditions prescribed, recommended, or suggested in the proposed labeling.
rules.verbatim_quote: Approval of any drug — for either a rare or a common disease or condition — must be based on substantial evidence of the drug's effectiveness for its intended use and sufficient information to conclude that the drug is safe for use under the conditions prescribed, recommended, or suggested in the proposed labeling.
rules.exceptions: The ODA does not create a different statutory standard for orphan drugs vs. drugs for common conditions

rules.rule_text: Sponsors should obtain evidence of effectiveness in an identified population from adequate and well-controlled studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors should obtain evidence of effectiveness in an identified population from adequate and well-controlled studies (see section VII., Evidence of Safety and Effectiveness).

rules.rule_text: Sponsors should evaluate early the depth and quality of existing natural history knowledge to determine if it is sufficient to inform their drug development programs.
rules.source_section: Section III.A. Considerations for Natural History Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: Although FDA does not require natural history studies, we advise sponsors to evaluate early the depth and quality of existing natural history knowledge to determine if it is sufficient to inform their drug development programs.
rules.exceptions: FDA does not require natural history studies

rules.rule_text: Natural history studies should be initiated in the earliest drug development planning stages and should be prospectively designed and protocol-driven.
rules.source_section: Section III.A. Considerations for Natural History Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: the need for prospectively designed, protocol-driven natural history studies initiated in the earliest drug development planning stages cannot be overemphasized.

rules.rule_text: Natural history studies should be conducted for sufficient duration to capture clinically meaningful outcomes and variability in the course of the disease.
rules.source_section: Section III.A. Considerations for Natural History Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: Conduct a study of sufficient duration to capture clinically meaningful outcomes and variability in the course of the disease.

rules.rule_text: Natural history studies should use standardized collection methods and medical terminology to enhance the value and usefulness of natural history study data.
rules.source_section: Section III.A. Considerations for Natural History Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: Use standardized collection methods and medical terminology to enhance the value and usefulness of natural history study data.

rules.rule_text: Natural history studies should include patients across a wide spectrum of disease severity and phenotypes rather than focus on a particular subtype.
rules.source_section: Section III.A. Considerations for Natural History Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: Include patients across a wide spectrum of disease severity and phenotypes, rather than focus on a particular subtype. Broad inclusion criteria can allow identification and better characterization of disease phenotypes for which therapy development may be more feasible or needed.

rules.rule_text: The analytic validity of a surrogate biomarker endpoint must be confirmed before starting the clinical trial.
rules.source_section: Section IV. Disease Pathophysiology, Clinical Manifestations, and Identification and Use of Biomarkers
rules.verbatim_quote: The analytic validity should be confirmed before starting the clinical trial. Analytical validation evaluates several factors including the following: Sensitivity of the assay; Specificity of the assay to measure the biomarker; Range of results that can be measured; Standardized methods of sample collection, shipment, and preparation; Reproducibility of the results.

rules.rule_text: The use of a surrogate endpoint requires demonstration of both analytical and clinical validation of the biomarker test.
rules.source_section: Section IV. Disease Pathophysiology, Clinical Manifestations, and Identification and Use of Biomarkers
rules.verbatim_quote: The use of a surrogate endpoint requires demonstration of analytical and clinical validation of the biomarker test.
rules.exceptions: Less well established surrogate endpoints considered reasonably likely to predict clinical benefit may be used for accelerated approval

rules.rule_text: Nonclinical studies are a mandated part of drug development. The goal of the nonclinical program is to provide evidence that the drug is reasonably safe to conduct the proposed clinical investigations.
rules.verbatim_quote: Nonclinical studies are a mandated part of drug development. The goal of the nonclinical program, which consists of in vitro and/or in vivo studies, is to provide evidence that the drug is "reasonably safe to conduct the proposed clinical investigations."

rules.rule_text: For serious or life-threatening diseases, clinical trials may proceed with a modified nonclinical development program only under limited circumstances, with sufficient justification, and when no specific safety concern is present.
rules.verbatim_quote: For serious or life-threatening diseases where current treatments, if any, are inadequate, clinical trials can often proceed with a modified nonclinical development program described in guidances on nonclinical studies. However, these trials may proceed only under limited circumstances, with sufficient justification, and when no specific safety concern is present.
rules.exceptions: Limited circumstances with sufficient justification and no specific safety concern

rules.rule_text: Sponsors should discuss any abbreviated or deferred nonclinical program with the FDA review division to obtain concurrence before implementing it.
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA strongly encourages a sponsor to discuss the proposed approach with the review division to obtain concurrence on any abbreviated or deferred nonclinical program that could support the proposed clinical trials.

rules.rule_text: When clinical trials are to be conducted in pediatric patients, proof-of-concept (POC) data is required to establish a prospect of direct benefit to the pediatric population.
rules.verbatim_quote: When clinical trials are to be conducted in pediatric patients, POC data is required to establish a prospect of direct benefit to the pediatric population.

rules.rule_text: FDA encourages the sponsor to communicate early in the drug development process with the review division to discuss an appropriate nonclinical development program for the investigational drug.
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA encourages the sponsor to communicate early in the drug development process with the review division to discuss an appropriate nonclinical development program for the investigational drug.

rules.rule_text: To define a trial endpoint, a sponsor should select a patient assessment to be used as an outcome measure and define when in the trial the patient would be assessed. Endpoint selection involves understanding the range and course of clinical manifestations, the clinical characteristics of the specific target population, and the aspects of the disease meaningful to the patient.
rules.verbatim_quote: To define a trial endpoint, a sponsor should select a patient assessment to be used as an outcome measure and define when in the trial the patient would be assessed.

rules.rule_text: If an adaptive design is under consideration, a thorough statistical analysis plan including key features of the trial design and preplanned analyses must be discussed with the review division before trial initiation.
rules.verbatim_quote: If an adaptive design is under consideration, a thorough statistical analysis plan including the key features of the trial design and preplanned analyses should be discussed with the review division before trial initiation.
cross_references: FDA Adaptive Design Clinical Trials for Drugs and Biologics (September 2018)

rules.rule_text: Sponsors considering development of novel clinical outcome assessments should identify and characterize these assessments early in their drug development programs. FDA advises sponsors to consider using or modifying existing assessment measures for the disease under study.
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors considering the development of novel clinical outcome assessments should identify and characterize these assessments early in their drug development programs. FDA advises sponsors to consider using or modifying existing assessment measures for the disease under study because evaluating novel measures is time consuming, with potential unexpected outcomes, and evaluations initiated late in the process could delay drug development.

rules.rule_text: One of the statutory requirements for drug marketing approval is substantial evidence that the drug will have its claimed effect. This requirement is the same for all drugs regardless of whether they are for common or rare diseases.
rules.verbatim_quote: One of the statutory requirements for drug marketing approval is "substantial evidence" that the drug will have its claimed effect. This requirement is the same for all drugs regardless of whether they are for common or rare diseases.

rules.rule_text: Adequate and well-controlled trial protocols must include: a clear statement of trial objectives with planned sample size rationale; a design that permits valid comparison with a control; well-defined methods of patient selection; methods that minimize bias in patient assignment; methods that minimize bias in trial conduct and outcome measures; well-defined and reliable methods of patient response assessment; and adequate statistical analysis methods.
rules.verbatim_quote: Design features of an adequate and well-controlled trial protocol include the following: A clear statement of the trial objectives, a statement and rationale regarding planned sample size, and a summary of the methods of analysis being used; A design that permits a valid comparison with a control that may be concurrent (e.g., placebo, standard of care, active treatment, dose comparison) or, in limited and special circumstances, historical; Methods of patient selection that are well defined and result in the selection of an appropriate population for trial; Methods that minimize bias in assigning patients to trial groups and ensure comparability between or among trial groups (e.g., randomization); Methods that minimize bias in trial conduct, outcome measures, and analysis (e.g., blinding techniques); Methods of assessment of patients' responses that are well defined and reliable (e.g., appropriate endpoints for the trial objectives); Methods of analysis adequate to assess effects of treatment (e.g., an appropriate statistical analysis plan).

rules.rule_text: Use of historical (external) controls is generally restricted to assessment of serious disease when there is an unmet medical need, a well-documented highly predictable disease course that can be objectively measured and verified, and an expected drug effect that is large, self-evident, and temporally closely associated with the intervention.
rules.verbatim_quote: This situation generally restricts use of historical control designs to assessment of serious disease when (1) there is an unmet medical need; (2) there is a well-documented, highly predictable disease course that can be objectively measured and verified, such as high and temporally predictable mortality; and (3) there is an expected drug effect that is large, self-evident, and temporally closely associated with the intervention.
rules.exceptions: Historical controls may be used when concurrent controls are impractical or unethical

rules.rule_text: Randomization of the first and all subsequently enrolled patients, including those in the earliest phases of clinical development, helps ensure that each patient's contribution is interpretable and avoids potentially misleading findings from open-label, single-arm, externally controlled trials.
rules.source_section: Section VII.B.2. Early randomization when feasible
rules.verbatim_quote: Randomization of the first and all subsequently enrolled patients, including those in the earliest phases of clinical development, helps ensure that each patient's contribution is interpretable, avoiding potentially misleading findings from open-label, single-arm, externally controlled trials.

rules.rule_text: The goal of safety evaluation during drug development is to characterize the drug's safety profile in a reasonable number of patients over a reasonable duration of time, consistent with the intended use of the drug. For rare diseases, 'reasonable' means consideration of feasibility challenges posed by the limited number of patients.
rules.verbatim_quote: The goal of safety evaluation during drug development is to characterize the drug's safety profile in a reasonable number of patients over a reasonable duration of time, consistent with the intended use of the drug. For the FDA, the term reasonable in the context of rare diseases means consideration of feasibility challenges posed by the limited number of patients with the disease.
rules.exceptions: ICH E1A expectations for population exposure for non-life-threatening conditions do not apply to the many rare diseases that are life threatening

rules.rule_text: Evidence-based decisions about feasibility of rare disease drug trial enrollment depend on accurately estimated disease prevalence. Prevalence estimates should include all phenotypic subtypes of a disorder anticipated to respond to the investigational drug.
rules.verbatim_quote: Evidence-based decisions about what is feasible in terms of rare disease drug trial enrollment depend on accurately estimated disease prevalence. Many rare diseases are genetic in origin and characterized by more than one phenotypic subtype (e.g., infantile, juvenile, adult). Prevalence estimates should include all phenotypic subtypes of a disorder anticipated to respond to the investigational drug.

rules.rule_text: For rare diseases, inclusion and exclusion criteria must not unnecessarily constrain patient eligibility in order to ensure adequate representation of the safety in the intended treatment population.
rules.verbatim_quote: For rare diseases, it is especially important that inclusion and exclusion criteria do not unnecessarily constrain patient eligibility for not only patient accrual but for an adequate representation of the safety in the intended treatment population.

rules.rule_text: For diseases that affect both children and adults, sponsors should explore early inclusion of pediatric patients in clinical studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Many rare diseases severely affect children, and for diseases that affect both children and adults, sponsors should explore early inclusion of pediatric patients in clinical studies.

rules.rule_text: From a safety evaluation perspective, sponsors should use a concurrent comparator arm design whenever ethically and practicably feasible to facilitate interpretation of adverse event causality.
rules.verbatim_quote: From a safety evaluation perspective, sponsors should use a concurrent comparator arm design (e.g., placebo, no treatment, standard of care, active drug, multiple doses), whenever ethically and practicably feasible, to facilitate interpretation of adverse event causality, especially with respect to the incidence and severity of adverse events that could be a manifestation of the disease under study.
rules.exceptions: When not ethically or practicably feasible

rules.rule_text: Sponsors should maintain communication with FDA as safety data accrue because timely discussion of potentially needed postmarketing studies or risk mitigation measures beyond labeling and routine pharmacovigilance facilitates submission of a complete marketing application.
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors should maintain communication with FDA as safety data accrue because timely discussion of potentially needed postmarketing studies or risk mitigation measures beyond labeling and routine pharmacovigilance facilitates submission of a complete marketing application.

rules.rule_text: Drug manufacturing should undergo development concurrently with clinical development. Review divisions encourage sponsors to discuss pharmaceutical quality development plans in early-phase meetings (such as pre-IND meetings) throughout drug development to decrease the potential for developmental or approval delays.
rules.verbatim_quote: Drug manufacturing should undergo development concurrently with clinical development. Review divisions encourage sponsors to discuss pharmaceutical quality development plans in early-phase (such as at pre-investigational new drug application (pre-IND) meetings) and throughout drug development to decrease the potential for developmental or approval delays related to drug manufacturing.

rules.rule_text: The sponsor must carefully assess any planned changes to the drug substance or drug product manufacturing process or drug product formulation at any phase of development to determine if the changes could directly or indirectly affect the safety or efficacy of the product.
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA recommends that the sponsor carefully assess any planned changes to the drug substance or drug product manufacturing process or drug product formulation at any phase of development to determine if the changes could directly or indirectly affect the safety or efficacy of the product.

rules.rule_text: Manufacturing changes should be made as early as feasible to allow time to evaluate the potential effect on drug safety and effectiveness and to minimize possible delays in development.
rules.verbatim_quote: To allow time to evaluate the potential effect of manufacturing changes on drug safety and effectiveness and to minimize possible delays in development, manufacturing changes should be made as early as feasible.

rules.rule_text: If significant differences are identified in drug characteristics after a manufacturing change compared to drug batches used in earlier nonclinical studies or clinical trials, then additional nonclinical studies and clinical trials may be needed.
rules.verbatim_quote: If significant differences are identified in drug characteristics after a manufacturing change compared to drug batches (or biological product lots) used in earlier nonclinical studies or clinical trials, then additional nonclinical studies and clinical trials may be needed because these differences can raise concerns that the knowledge gained from the earlier studies will not apply to further use of the drug.

rules.rule_text: Most rare diseases are serious or life-threatening disorders with unmet medical needs, and drugs treating these diseases may qualify for one or more expedited programs, including fast track designation, breakthrough therapy designation, priority review designation, and accelerated approval.
rules.verbatim_quote: Most rare diseases are serious or life-threatening disorders with unmet medical needs and, therefore, drugs treating these diseases may qualify for one or more expedited programs. FDA encourages sponsors to consider these programs, which include fast track designation, breakthrough therapy designation, priority review designation, and accelerated approval.
cross_references: FDA Expedited Programs for Serious Conditions — Drugs and Biologics (May 2014)

rules.rule_text: About half of the people affected by rare diseases are children. Sponsors should include pediatric patients with rare diseases in premarketing clinical studies to develop data on the full range of people with the disease. FDA strongly encourages sponsors to study the drug in all relevant pediatric populations, birth to younger than 17 years of age.
rules.verbatim_quote: According to estimates, about half of the people affected by rare diseases are children. Therefore, conducting studies to evaluate drugs in pediatric patients is critical for determining the safety and efficacy of medications for many rare diseases. In general, sponsors should include pediatric patients with rare diseases in premarketing clinical studies to develop data on the full range of people with the disease. FDA strongly encourages sponsors to study the drug in all relevant pediatric populations, birth to younger than 17 years of age, so that the drug can be properly and completely labeled for pediatric use.

rules.rule_text: When developing an investigational drug for a rare disease, FDA encourages sponsors to meet with the relevant drug review division. FDA provides formal advice through milestone meetings including pre-IND meeting and end of phase 1 meeting.
rules.verbatim_quote: When developing an investigational drug for a rare disease, FDA encourages sponsors to meet with the relevant drug review division supporting development of that particular drug. FDA's feedback to sponsors may result in more efficient drug development. At the sponsor's request, FDA will, if possible, provide advice on specific matters relating to an IND, including advice on the adequacy of data to support an investigational plan, the design of a clinical trial, and whether proposed investigations are likely to produce the data and information needed to meet requirements for a marketing application. FDA provides formal advice through milestone meetings (e.g., pre-IND meeting, end of phase 1 meeting).

rules.rule_text: The Orphan Drug Act defines a rare disease or condition as one affecting fewer than 200,000 people in the United States. The ODA created a process for orphan drug designation making sponsors eligible for certain financial incentives including tax credits, waiver of PDUFA fees, and 7-year market exclusivity.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Orphan Drug Act (the ODA) generally defines a rare disease or condition as one affecting fewer than 200,000 people in the United States. Most rare diseases, however, affect far fewer people. The ODA created a process for the Agency to designate a drug as a drug for a rare disease or condition. The sponsor of a drug holding orphan drug designation may be eligible for certain financial incentives intended to help make developing drugs for small numbers of patients financially viable.
rules.exceptions: ODA also defines a rare disease as any condition affecting more than 200,000 persons in the US for which there is no reasonable expectation that the cost of developing and making available a drug for the condition will be recovered from sales
